events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment JSON directly. I’m treating the shared-neutral AFCI nuisance trip as a likely wiring/configuration defect that has to be isolated and corrected before energizing or continuing functional troubleshooting.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /tmp/answer.tmp <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-progress\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 45,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.86,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [
    \"verify-work-order-and-OEM-info\",
    \"de-energize-and-lockout-affected-circuits\",
    \"confirm-absence-of-voltage\",
    \"identify-all-conductors-and-shared-neutral-condition\",
    \"correct-neutral-and-AFCI-circuit-configuration\",
    \"restore-power-for-controlled-functional-test\",
    \"test-AFCI-GFCI-operation-and-document-results\"
  ],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"AFCI nuisance-trip diagnosis indicates a shared neutral or mismatched neutral return path on AFCI-protected circuitry.\",
    \"Continuing energized troubleshooting or resetting without isolating the neutral fault can leave required AFCI/GFCI protection unreliable.\",
    \"Shared or crossed neutrals on AFCI circuits are a wiring defect unless handled with an approved common-trip or listed multiwire branch-circuit configuration.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop functional troubleshooting that depends on repeated reset or energized operation.\",
    \"De-energize and verify absence of voltage on all affected branch-circuit conductors, including the shared neutral.\",
    \"Trace hot and neutral conductors to confirm circuit pairing and any multiwire branch-circuit arrangement.\",
    \"Correct the shared or crossed neutral condition using listed AFCI/GFCI equipment appropriate to the circuit configuration before retesting.\",
    \"After correction, perform required AFCI and GFCI functional tests and document results.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"AFCI/GFCI troubleshooting must first establish a safe, code-compliant conductor configuration. A shared or crossed neutral can cause nuisance tripping and can defeat or destabilize required protection, so the sequence must stop at isolation, verification, and correction before further functional testing or returning the circuit to service.\",
  \"references\": [\"NEC 210.8\", \"NEC 210.12\"]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.tmp /app/answer.json
cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Jul  5 06:10 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:28 ..
